title: Questions about Gendo and EoE
wikitext:
[]: Re: Misato after 3I             Quote Post by vinska » Sun Jul 03, 2016 4:33 pm Chainer3000 wrote: Is it possible Misato never joined in the human instrumentally project? She, like Gendo, was killed prior to the actual Rei-soul-grabbing, and that seemed to have excluded Gendo from joining in on the LCL party. I always thought it remained a bit ambiguous as to whether or not she took part.

[]: Questions about Gendo and EoE.             Quote Post by NONAME » Mon Jul 04, 2016 3:32 pm Hi everyone! I have watched the movie a few times and there's always a scene that I don't seem to understand: the conversation between Gendo and Yui... and Kaworu. Well, as we know, everyone else was seeing the person that they cared the most right before turning into LCL. But in the case of Gendo (who did not turn into LCL, I think), he saw three persons. It makes sense that he saw Rei and Yui, they were very important to him, but why Kaworu ? That's my first question. Because he and Gendo were not close, and they never spoke to each other before. To me, that doesn't make sense. The other thing that confuses me is when he get killed by Eva Unit 01. They are many theories that assume it was Shinji who killed him because he hated it. But honestly, I don't think he was capable of that, no matter of how much he hated it, not after his 'confession'. The other option is that he was killed by Yui because of the pain he caused to his son. I don't see Yui as 'purity' anymore, but I don't think she would have killed him either. I think she still loves him, though, and I don't see why she would choose to reject just him from instrumentality. It wouldn't be possible that scene was a kind of desire of Gendo, for his attitude, instead of what really happened? Because I still find it too surreal. However, I have read a theory that said Yui took away Gendo soul to live with him in the Eva forever. This makes a little of more sense, but it's still improbable, though. What do you guys think? P.S: Sorry if I made any mistake, English is not my native language.

[]: Re: Questions about Gendo and EoE.             Quote Post by NemZ » Mon Jul 04, 2016 8:12 pm For the first, when Rei took the Adam embryo from Gendo along with his hand she effectively absorbed a part of Kaworu. She then merged with Yui above the geofront, so when that confrontation with Gendo took place later all three of them were connected. What actually happened to Gendo afterwards and who was responsible (or not) isn't clear. You just have to find your own answers for that because the movie doesn't offer any certainty there.

[]: Re: Misato after 3I             Quote Post by Bagheera » Mon Jul 04, 2016 9:07 pm Chainer3000 wrote: Is it possible Misato never joined in the human instrumentally project? She, like Gendo, was killed prior to the actual Rei-soul-grabbing, and that seemed to have excluded Gendo from joining in on the LCL party. I always thought it remained a bit ambiguous as to whether or not she took part.

[]: Re: Misato after 3I             Quote Post by Sachi » Tue Jul 05, 2016 2:00 am ^What evidence is there that Gendo was present during Instrumentlaity in EoE? Misato, most certainly, but Gendo has no lines after his "retribution" and he isn't talked about either.

[]: Re: Misato after 3I             Quote Post by Bagheera » Tue Jul 05, 2016 2:14 am Sachi wrote: ^What evidence is there that Gendo was present during Instrumentlaity in EoE? Misato, most certainly, but Gendo has no lines after his "retribution" and he isn't talked about either.

[]: Re: Misato after 3I             Quote Post by Sachi » Tue Jul 05, 2016 2:31 am Well, there was Pre-3I weirdness going on before the retribution scene. His death occurs during the same scene as everybody else getting tanged, yet he doesn't dissolve the same way the others do. He's instead chopped in half and left that way, dead with his body still intact, and his soul likely still within it. Plus, how is this scene at all a retribution if he is rewarded by being allowed into Instrumentality? He's being punished for being a dick by getting excluded from Instrumentality, not welcomed into it.

[]: Re: Misato after 3I             Quote Post by Bagheera » Tue Jul 05, 2016 2:45 am Sachi wrote: Well, there was Pre-3I weirdness going on before the retribution scene. His death occurs during the same scene as everybody else getting tanged, yet he doesn't dissolve the same way the others do. He's instead chopped in half and left that way, dead with his body still intact, and his soul likely still within it. Plus, how is this scene at all a retribution if he is rewarded by being allowed into Instrumentality? He's being punished for being a dick by getting excluded from Instrumentality, not welcomed into it.

[]: Re: Misato after 3I             Quote Post by Reichu » Tue Jul 05, 2016 2:57 am Why would his soul be any less within the half that got eaten than in the half that stayed behind? And he WAS somehow devoured by Eva-01 -- as always, the specifics of how matter much less than the fact we saw it happen and therefore it was a thing capable of happening -- so he was not merely killed, he was taken within . Chronicle Side B actually uses this interpretation in its final character summary section. Doesn't mean it's true, but it was nice to see one of my old standbys seconded in print. :p My copy's not with me ATM, so perhaps someone else can fish up the relevant bit?

[]: Re: Questions about Gendo and EoE.             Quote Post by Reichu » Tue Jul 05, 2016 3:03 am Before Eva-01 appears, Yui curiously changes from a pink blouse to a purple turtleneck, providing a sort of transition, if you will. The Eva itself is also a visual callback to the end of episode 19, when Yui was unambiguously running the show. (Also references Shinji's confrontation with the Eva in episode 25, where he addresses her as his mom.) A connection to Shinji seems tenuous at best. As for why Yui would violently kill Gendo, remember that she's more or less taking the place of Rei. The way Rei appears to those she spirits away is not a reflection upon her own feelings, but those of the people being liberated. Logically, Yui follows suit, and provides Gendo something he desired in his own heart. Wishing for a violent rather than peaceful death is in character, given his abundant self loathing.

[]: Re: Misato after 3I             Quote Post by Sachi » Tue Jul 05, 2016 3:25 am Bagheera wrote: This approach doesn't make sense. You're looking at what happened to him as though it were taking place in the real world when we know that can't be the case since Unit 01 is not in TD at the time. There is simply no way for that scene to happen as depicted in reality -- it must be something like P3II because how else do you account for Unit 01 and the Reis?

[]: Re: Questions about Gendo and EoE.             Quote Post by Reichu » Tue Jul 05, 2016 3:47 am Only half of his body is left behind. Note that when Rei does it, the WHOLE body is left. All that matters for HIP is rounding up the soul. Everyone else is turned into LCL so their souls can be transported easily. In Gendo's case, it could be argued that this isn't required because Eva-01 shows up in person and eats Gendo's soul. To put this into perspective a little, consider the significance of the last time Yui ate something.

[]: Re: Questions about Gendo and EoE.             Quote Post by Sachi » Tue Jul 05, 2016 4:04 am A ha! I finally found the main thread on this subject: thread/1840/Gendos-Mysterious-Induction-or-How-I-Ate-My-Husband I'm gonna need to skim through this a bit.

[]: Re: Misato after 3I             Quote Post by pwhodges » Tue Jul 05, 2016 5:07 am Reichu wrote: My copy's not with me ATM, so perhaps someone else can fish up the relevant bit?

[]: Re: Questions about Gendo and EoE.             Quote Post by Reichu » Tue Jul 05, 2016 5:26 am There's something else from later in the book IIRC, on the page that has the screenshot of GNR's disembodied arm with all the green crosses. (Should note in advance that Chronicle's HIP-related references to LCL are really wonky and they're not necessarily implying here that Gendo's body turned into LCL. I think they might actually mean LCL Sea , since that's where the souls end up.)

[]: Re: Questions about Gendo and EoE.             Quote Post by pwhodges » Tue Jul 05, 2016 5:31 am Side B, page 096 wrote: When Rei merged with Lilith and revealed herself to all mankind, Gendoh was absorbed into EVA-01 and finally reunited with Yui, the love of his life.

[]: Re: Questions about Gendo and EoE.             Quote Post by pwhodges » Tue Jul 05, 2016 5:36 am On that page I also notice: Side B, page 096 wrote: As Lilith's form fell apart, she was surrounded by countless glowing crosses. Each of these crosses represented a life that had not become part of the perfect singular organism that instrumentality was supposed to produce.

[]: Re: Questions about Gendo and EoE.             Quote Post by Reichu » Tue Jul 05, 2016 6:10 am That comment is a bit baffling and I can't think of anything in the film that begins to support it. The crosses are plainly seen early on to be something quite separate from souls, which we KNOW to represent "lives". They generate on cue, as the AATF spreads, to launch a multitude of souls into the stream heading for Lilith. I assume they're just extensions of the AATF itself, and they accordingly dissipate when HIP ends and the AATF goes bye bye. Incidentally, this is one of many bits of inexplicable oddness in Chronicle I would have listified in the dedicated Side A/B thread if anyone had actually responded to my post. :p Now I see your edit -- "single entity in the form of LCL", seriously, what the bloody hell are they talking about? This is Evangelion, not Parasite Eve.

[]: Re: Misato after 3I             Quote Post by Bagheera » Tue Jul 05, 2016 7:04 am Sachi wrote: They're transition guides, Unit-01 included.

[]: Re: Questions about Gendo and EoE.             Quote Post by robersora » Tue Jul 05, 2016 7:43 am I guess the in incongruence arises out of narrative convenience.

She, like Gendo, was killed prior to the actual Rei-soul-grabbing, and that seemed to have excluded Gendo from joining in on the LCL party.
